verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted cytokine that primarily accumulates in ECM as latent complex. However, latent TGFB1 is stably anchored to the plasma membrane in specific cell types (Tregs, activated B cells, macrophages) via covalent disulfide-bond tethering to transmembrane docking receptors GARP and LRRC33, or via thrombospondin-1 binding to cell-surface receptors. The protein body reaches the outer leaflet by its own mechanism (via LAP-GARP disulfide linkage) but only under documented cell-state/activation conditions, making it contextual surface-accessible.
